# Copilot instructions
Write code well, without bugs, using modern standards and practices.
This is a project in Java, so using Java conventions and practices is very important.

## Best practices:
- Always use final
- Always close scanners and resources after using them
- Always prefer Object Oriented data structures like maps and sets to arrays and matrices
- Always catch possible exceptions that arise from wrong user input or error cases
- Always use var where type is inferrable
- Do not use an array to store a singular value
- Import classes when necessary, don't use the full package name when it's not needed
- Avoid magic numbers, always store them in constants unless it's 0 or 1
- When writing tests try to make a setup that runs before each test instead of repeating lines of code
